# `ai-strategy.ron` — field reference

<!-- GENERATED by `cargo run -p dc_data_docs` (dronecom #2815). Do not edit: field docs live as /// comments on the Rust structs. -->

Data: [`config/ai-strategy.ron`](../config/ai-strategy.ron)

## `StrategyConfig`

Thresholds the strategy assessment uses to choose an objective + posture.

### `horizon_secs` : f32

Commitment window (seconds): a chosen objective is held this long before
a scheduled re-assessment, unless an emergency (a threatened carrier)
forces an earlier switch. Hysteresis — stops per-tick posture flapping.

### `threat_range_m` : f32

A carrier counts as threatened when its nearest fresh enemy contact is
within this planar range (metres) and closing.

### `press_force_floor` : u32

Minimum count of own armed units to adopt a `Press` posture; below it the
main-effort carrier probes (scouts cautiously) instead of committing.

### `press_advance_step_m` : f32

Forward step (metres) a `Press` carrier advances toward the objective
focus per `MoveCarrier` task. The carrier closes the distance to the
focus, capped so a single hop never overshoots it.

### `probe_advance_step_m` : f32

Forward step (metres) a `Probe` carrier advances — shorter than
`press_advance_step_m`: cautious scouting forward, not a commitment.

### `press_engage_reach_mult` : f32

Multiplier on the engagement advisor's effective reach
(`max_engage_distance`) under a `Press` carrier. `Hold`/`Probe` keep the
unscaled reach. Lifts the static offense cage (AC #3).

### `advance_standoff_mult` : f32

Standoff distance an advancing carrier holds from the objective focus, as
a fraction of `max_engage_distance`. `< 1.0` closes the carrier *inside*
the air wing's strike envelope (with margin) so a launched strike
reliably reaches the focus, rather than parking at the envelope edge where
the focus is marginally out of reach.

### `probe_scout_forward_bias` : f32

Extra weight on the forward (toward-objective) geometric bias when the
patrol advisor orders stale cells under `Probe`/`ControlMap`. Added to
the existing threat + home-axis prior so scouting pushes toward the
enemy axis, not just home sectors (folds #316).

### `press_offense_mult` : f32

Multiplier on offensive task value (Engage, strike-carrier `MoveCarrier`)
when the team posture is `Press`. `> 1.0` raises offense priority.

### `hold_defense_mult` : f32

Multiplier on defensive task value (`DefendCarrier`) when the team posture
is `Hold`. `> 1.0` raises defense priority.

### `probe_scout_mult` : f32

Multiplier on scouting task value (Patrol, Classify, Monitor) when the
team posture is `Probe`. `> 1.0` raises scout priority.

### `base_worth_offense` : f32

Absolute base worth of an offensive task (Engage, strike `MoveCarrier`).
The auction multiplies this by the situational score and posture
multiplier to get a value comparable ACROSS task categories.

### `base_worth_defense` : f32

Absolute base worth of a defensive task (`DefendCarrier`).

### `base_worth_scout` : f32

Absolute base worth of a scouting task (Patrol, Classify, Monitor).

### `base_worth_neutral` : f32

Absolute base worth of a neutral task (`RecoverUnit`, hold/withdraw moves).

### `belief_useful_radius_m` : f32

Reachable-disk radius (metres) at which an unobserved enemy belief's
confidence fades to zero: `reachable = max_speed · elapsed`, `confidence
= 1 − reachable / useful_radius`. Larger = the AI remembers contacts over
a wider area of positional uncertainty before forgetting them. A fast
contact crosses this radius — and is evicted — far sooner than a slow one.

### `belief_confidence_floor` : f32

Confidence floor (`0..1`): a decaying belief below this is evicted (the AI
has forgotten the contact).
